title: ARIA properties in DOM elements should include valid values
description: ARIA properties in DOM elements should have valid valuesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityreliabilityCode SmellreactaccessibilityWhy is this an issue?How to fixMore InfoARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) attributes are used to enhance the accessibility of web content and web applications. These attributes provide additional information about an element’s role, state, properties, and values to assistive technologies like screen readers.This rule checks that the values of ARIA attributes "aria-*" in DOM elements are valid.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.3Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvail
recommendation: Ensure ARIA attributes have valid values according to the ARIA specification.
impact: Accessibility barrier for screen readers and assistive technologies
